# Fitting the Multiverse

`fit_multiverse()` applies a fitting function to every row of the grid and
returns a tidy table, one row per specification. Because the fits are
independent, `.parallel = TRUE` spreads them across cores with `furrr`.

```r
results <- fit_multiverse(specs, function(spec) {
  f <- reformulate(c("exposure", spec$adjusters[[1]]), "outcome")
  broom::tidy(lm(f, data = df), conf.int = TRUE)
}, .parallel = TRUE)
```

*(Efficiency notes on caching and design-matrix reuse in preparation.)*
